name: EPGeditARR
description:
EPGeditARR cleans, transforms, and organizes your EPG data.

EPG TRANSFORMATION: Strip episode codes, broadcast flags, quality tags, and other noise from program titles and descriptions using regex or find/replace rules. Virtual copies of your sources are kept in sync automatically after every refresh — originals are never touched.

FILL EPG: Generate repeating placeholder schedules for channels with no EPG data so every channel shows something in your TV guide.

SIRIUSXM TOOLS: Match channels to SiriusXM's Wikipedia lineup for real descriptions, sort your entire channel group into official lineup order, and automatically defer seasonal holiday channels to the end of the list until they go active.

RULE DESIGNER: Build and test rules visually at https://jstevenscl.github.io/epgeditarr/designer.html — pick from preset libraries or write your own, with live before/after preview.
author: jstevenscl

actions.label: Setup Virtual EPGs
actions.description: Create virtual EPG sources for all enabled sources, run the initial transform, and reassign channels. Run once after enabling sources and configuring rules.

actions.label: Apply Rules Now
actions.description: Re-run the transformation on all enabled sources. Use this after changing rules without waiting for the next EPG refresh.

actions.label: Scan for Channels Without EPG
actions.description: List all channels that have no EPG data assigned, grouped by channel group. Shows which groups are targeted by Fill EPG.

actions.label: Fill EPG
actions.description: Generate a repeating EPG schedule for all channels in the configured Fill Groups that have no EPG data. Optionally enriches matched SiriusXM channels with real descriptions.

actions.label: Sort Channels
actions.description: Reorder all channels in your Fill Groups to match the SiriusXM official lineup. Assigns sequential channel numbers starting from Sort Start Number. Unmatched channels are placed at the end. Requires SiriusXM Enrichment data.

actions.label: Fill EPG & Sort Channels
actions.description: Run Fill EPG and Sort in sequence — generates schedules then reorders all channels in your Fill Groups to match SiriusXM's lineup.

actions.label: Refresh Channel Data
actions.description: Force an immediate refresh of the SiriusXM channel list from Wikipedia, overwriting the local cache.

actions.label: Test Rule
actions.description: Apply the Pattern and Replacement from the Rule Tester section to the Test Text and show matches and the transformed result.

actions.label: Sample Source Data
actions.description: Show raw title, sub-title, and description values from each enabled source so you can write accurate rules.

actions.label: Preview Rule Changes
actions.description: Dry run across all enabled sources — show which programs would change and before/after values. No data is modified.

actions.label: Show Status
actions.description: Show all EPG sources, which are enabled, virtual EPG program counts, and configured rules.

actions.label: Remove Virtual EPGs
actions.description: Delete all virtual EPG sources and Fill EPG, and reassign channels back to their original sources.

confirm.message: This will delete all virtual EPGs (including Fill EPG) and reassign channels back to their original sources. Continue?
